what_it_does: Bash wrapper that execs the real Claude binary with the Company channel MCP config and the development-channels server flag when both exist, passing all arguments through; otherwise it falls back to a plain launch.
observations: Auto-joins the 'Company cross-session channel fabric' on every interactive launch
Uses the full filesystem path to the real Claude binary to avoid recursion through the alias
Wired as an interactive-shell-only alias in ~/.bashrc
Includes a fail-safe fallback that never blocks launch if the config or binary is missing
Replaces the wrapper process with exec
Comment notes that connect-time self-registration already existed in company_channel.mjs
standouts: Hardcoded full-path exec prevents alias recursion
Fail-safe fallback guarantees launch even if fabric config is absent
Interactive-only alias keeps non-interactive worker calls on the plain binary
Automatically enables a 'dangerously' prefixed development flag
conventions: Follows a thin-wrapper pattern with exec and "$@" passthrough
Uses fail-safe rather than fail-closed behavior
Uses full absolute paths instead of PATH resolution
Heavy header comment explaining rationale and reversibility
concerns: Hardcodes /home/tim paths, making it user- and machine-specific
Alias globally overrides the claude command for interactive shells
Fallback exec "${REAL_CLAUDE:-claude}" could recurse if REAL_CLAUDE is unset and claude is still aliased
notes: A reversible, fail-safe interactive alias wrapper that transparently attaches Claude to the Company channel fabric while preserving normal launch behavior.
questions: What is the exact structure and trust model of channel.mcp.json?
What does server:company-channel resolve to and where is it defined?
What does company_channel.mjs writeReg do and where does it live?
Has the ~/.bashrc alias already been installed or is this only the proposed wrapper?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it wraps interactive launches with the channel fabric config and falls back to plain launches when the config is missing.
apparent_themes: Boundary between interactive and non-interactive usage
intention_signals: It appears designed to make every interactive Claude session automatically participate in the Company channel fabric without changing Tim's normal workflow, while keeping autonomous/non-interactive invocations untouched.
novelty: It is a shell-alias shim that avoids recursion by calling the real binary via full path and auto-enables a development-channel server flag, rather than modifying the Claude binary or global config.
contribution: Provides a transparent, reversible launch shim that attaches interactive sessions to the channel fabric, preserves full argument passthrough, and keeps a fallback path that does not block launches.
summary_for_embedding: Bash wrapper script that execs the real Claude binary with a company channel MCP config and a development-channels server flag, passing through all arguments and falling back to a plain launch if the config or binary is missing.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to auto-join Tim's interactive Claude sessions to the Company cross-session channel fabric without blocking launches or affecting non-interactive worker calls.
